div.p: Algorithm Parameters: Max Degree ($M$) = 2. Objective: Spatial pruning to prevent graph "clumping" and ensure long-range traversal. Core Logic: Select candidate $C$ only if it is closer to Query $Q$ than to any already selected neighbor $R$.

ul.li: Sort: Ascending by distance to Q.
Context: C1 and C2 are spatially correlated (close to each other).
Result: $0.5 < 1.2 \implies$ REJECT C2 is "occluded" by C1.

div.p: Result Set: {C1, C3} Logic: C3 (d=1.5) is preferred over C2 (d=1.2) because it is farther from C1 than it is from Q, ensuring a broad search frontier. This maintains the "Small World" property of the graph.
